summary: Comprehensive testing of TLS ARENA tournament management system. 23/23 backend pytest tests passing. All key frontend flows (home, login, admin dashboard, F1 leaderboard, TV display, tournament detail) load correctly with no console errors.

minor.issue: Endpoints only accept UUID, not slug. The review request expects 'GET /api/f1/challenges/f1-winter-championship/leaderboard' to work with slug. Currently returns 404. The detail endpoint /api/f1/challenges/{slug_or_id} supports slug, but downstream endpoints don't. Recommend resolving slug to id at the start of leaderboard/championship/times/export.csv handlers.

Tournament action endpoints accept only UUID. Only GET /api/tournaments/{slug_or_id} supports slug. Inconsistency: list view links by slug but actions need UUID. Frontend currently works because it has the UUID after fetching detail, but this is fragile and inconsistent.

Brute-force counter is keyed by request.client.host:email. Behind k8s ingress, multiple proxy IPs (e.g. 10.64.130.10 and 10.64.128.135) round-robin requests, so per-identifier count rarely reaches threshold of 7. Required ~14-16 attempts in testing instead of 7 to trigger 429. Fix: use X-Forwarded-For (first hop) or just email as identifier.

issues: Track thumbnails show broken-image placeholder (img alt 'Silverstone' renders without src). Backend f1_tracks records have no image_url so this is data, but frontend should hide when no src or use a fallback.

action_items: Add slug-or-id resolution at top of F1 leaderboard/championship/times/export.csv handlers to match the public URL contract (slug expected per spec).
Add slug-or-id resolution to tournament action endpoints (generate-bracket, register, checkin, bracket, standings, registrations).
Fix brute-force identifier to use X-Forwarded-For (first IP) instead of request.client.host so the threshold of 7 actually triggers behind the k8s ingress.
Hide F1 track when track.image_url is empty (currently shows broken image placeholder).
critical_code_review_comments: tournament_routes and f1_routes are inconsistent: detail GET accepts slug_or_id but all action endpoints require UUID. This is a UX/API hazard - a developer integrating against /api/f1/challenges/{slug}/leaderboard (per docs) gets 404.
Brute-force protection is effectively bypassable due to ingress IP rotation. Should also consider a global per-email rate limit independent of source IP.

/api/auth/register and /api/auth/login do NOT return access_token in the response body (they only set httpOnly cookies). Tests had to use cookies. Frontend works because it relies on cookies + /me, but mobile/non-cookie clients would have no way to obtain a bearer token. Consider returning access_token in body for completeness.
Email is stored & matched lowercased on login but original casing is preserved nowhere - that's fine. Just ensure docs note emails are case-insensitive.
test_credentials: Superadmin admin@thelionsquad.at / TLSAdmin2026! and demo player leon_king@demo.thelionsquad.at / demo123 both verified working.
seed_data_creation: Tests created several TEST_ users via /api/auth/register and a few test games (cleaned up). Player count grew from 21 to ~26 due to test users - non-blocking, all asserts use >=21.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite is at /app/backend/tests/test_tls_arena_api.py with 23 passing tests covering auth, games, tournaments+bracket gen, F1 leaderboard/championship/csv/add-time, admin dashboard, role enforcement, brute force, registration+checkin, user PATCH. All green. Frontend manually verified: home page, login flow, admin dashboard KPIs (26 players, 5 teams, 1 active tournament), F1 detail with track switcher and Silverstone leaderboard sorted ASC, TV/beamer mode with podium top-3 + ranked list, Mario Kart tournament page with 16 participants. No console errors observed. Open issues are non-blocking but should be addressed: (a) slug support for F1 + tournament action endpoints, (b) brute force ingress-IP issue, (c) broken track image placeholder.
rca of the issue: Slug 404 RCA: /api/f1/challenges/{cid}/leaderboard does `db.f1_challenges.find_one({"id": cid})` directly without slug fallback. Same pattern in tournament_routes.py for action endpoints. Fix: at the top of each handler, do `c = await db.x.find_one({"$or": [{"id": cid}, {"slug": cid}]})`. Brute-force RCA: client.host = ingress proxy pod IP which varies; mongo doc shows two IPs interleaved. Fix in auth_routes.py _check_brute_force/_record_failed to derive client_ip from `request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for', request.client.host).split(',')[0].strip()`.
